content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND from Topic A) can be re-purposed to prioritize which unlabeled data points an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, creating a 'curiosity gate' that only retains high-uncertainty samples that push the agent's current understanding forward. This solves the key 2024-2025 SOTA challenge of active learning agent memory bloat from storing redundant low-value unlabeled data.
